title: BDAG locked display rounds away fractional balances

evidence.quote:
<StatCard
        label={t("bdagLocked")}
        value={stats?.bdagLocked ?? 0}
        suffix=" BDAG"
        isLive={isLive}
        loading={loading}
      />

reasoning: The fetched BDAG balance is explicitly converted from wei to a fractional number, but the shared count-up hook rounds every animation frame to an integer before StatCard formats the BDAG value. A real balance such as 0.5 BDAG will render as 1.00 BDAG when animation completes, and 12.34 BDAG will render as 12 BDAG. This materially misreports the live locked value.
reproduction: Mock client.getBalance to resolve to 500000000000000000n and render LiveStats until the BDAG card animation completes; the card displays 1.00 BDAG instead of 0.50 BDAG.
recommendation: Preserve decimal precision for fractional metrics. For example, let useCountUp return a floating-point value or add a precision option, then only round integer-only stats such as escrow count, chain id, and exploits.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No tests were included for this feature, and there is no coverage asserting formatting of fractional BDAG balances.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a component test for StatCard or LiveStats that supplies a fractional BDAG value and asserts the displayed amount retains the expected decimal precision after animation completes.
minimumFixScope: frontend/src/components/live-stats.tsx: adjust useCountUp/StatCard formatting so BDAG values are not integer-rounded before display.
